fact: Founded in 1911 by Hull House reformers, SGA helps children, families and communities facing great challenges to realize their potential.

After merging with the Scholarship Association for Jewish Children, the organization became the Scholarship and Guidance Association (SGA), expanding its mission to address emotional and social challenges.

Joseph Pedott — the advertising legend behind the Chia Pet and The Clapper — was a teenage runaway in Chicago who turned to SGA Youth & Family Services (then known as Scholarship and Guidance) for help. SGA provided him with a counselor and funds to cover living expenses, helping him finish high school and attend the University of Illinois. He later donated more than $1 million to SGA and established a nonprofit with more than $10 million in assets, making him SGA's most generous donor. Pedott died June 22, 2023, at age 91.

Youth earn $16.50 per hour and receive career coaching and academic support. (SGA Youth and Family Services – Youth Employment Program, Lake County; listed on 211 Lake County directory, resource ID 4757392, confirmed live as of June 2026.)

SGA Youth and Family Services operates a program in Roseland listed on myChiMyFuture.org under the city-wide 'Cycle Breakers' initiative, tagged #CBMentoring #RestorativeJustice #CycleBreakers, serving youth ages 14–18 for free (in-person), under their SCAN (Service Coordination and Navigation) umbrella. Program dates listed as 01/01/2024–01/01/2027.
